collisions.paper: Sun et al., 2025. Investigating the Role of Weight Decay in Enhancing Nonconvex SGD. CVPR 2025.
collisions.overlap: Direct foundation paper introducing the alignment quantity delta_T for fixed weight decay generalization theory in nonconvex SGD

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2025. Cautious Weight Decay. arXiv:2510.12402.
collisions.overlap: Both use gradient-parameter alignment to modulate weight decay. CWD uses binary sign-based alignment at coordinate level; AADWD uses continuous cosine similarity at global level. CWD lacks convergence rate theory.

collisions.paper: Ghiasi et al., 2023. Improving Robustness with Adaptive Weight Decay. NeurIPS 2023.
collisions.overlap: Both dynamically adjust weight decay during training. Ghiasi uses gradient/weight norm ratio for robustness; AADWD uses gradient-parameter cosine similarity for generalization with convergence theory.

collisions.paper: Xie et al., 2023. On the Overlooked Pitfalls of Weight Decay — Scheduled Weight Decay (SWD). NeurIPS 2023.
collisions.overlap: Both dynamically schedule weight decay during training. SWD uses gradient norm statistics for Adam; AADWD uses alignment signal for SGD with convergence theory.

collisions.paper: Nakamura & Hong, 2019. Adaptive Weight Decay for Deep Neural Networks. IEEE Access.
collisions.overlap: Per-parameter adaptive weight decay based on gradient magnitude. Different mechanism (no alignment), no convergence theory.

collisions.paper: He et al., 2025. AlphaDecay: Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. arXiv:2506.14562.
collisions.overlap: Module-wise adaptive weight decay based on spectral analysis. Static, no per-step dynamics, no alignment, no convergence theory.

collisions.paper: Jiang et al., 2025. GALA: Online Learning-guided Learning Rate Adaptation via Gradient Alignment. arXiv:2506.08419.
collisions.overlap: Uses gradient alignment to adapt learning rate (not weight decay). Gradient-to-gradient alignment between consecutive steps, not gradient-to-weight alignment.

collisions.paper: Chou, 2025. Correction of Decoupled Weight Decay. arXiv:2512.08217.
collisions.overlap: Studies optimal WD scaling (lambda proportional to gamma^2) for AdamW. Related to AADWD's convergence condition lambda_t = O(gamma_t^2) but different focus.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Three-layer theoretical contribution is genuinely novel: (1) first convergence theory for time-varying SGDW in nonconvex setting, (2) cumulative contraction stability framework replacing worst-case alignment with trajectory-weighted average, (3) stochastic proxy transfer theorem. Closest competitor CWD uses binary sign-based alignment without convergence rate theory. SWD and Ghiasi et al. use different signals (gradient norms, not alignment).

collisions.paper: Sun et al., 2025. Investigating the Role of Weight Decay in Enhancing Nonconvex SGD. CVPR 2025.
collisions.overlap: Introduces the alignment quantity delta_t but does not systematically characterize its behavior across architectures/datasets as a primary contribution.

collisions.paper: Wan et al., 2021. Spherical Motion Dynamics. NeurIPS 2021.
collisions.overlap: Studies angular dynamics of weights under SGD+WD for normalized networks. Different theoretical framework, does not characterize gradient-parameter cosine similarity.

collisions.paper: Kunin et al., 2020. Neural Mechanics: Symmetry and Broken Conservation Laws in Deep Learning Dynamics.
collisions.overlap: Studies weight-gradient relationships through Lagrangian mechanics. Different framework, does not characterize delta_t dynamics.

candidates.differentiation_notes: No paper systematically characterizes delta_t dynamics as a primary contribution. However, the novelty ceiling is moderate for a purely empirical study. Viable as backup if the front-runner's theory proves too hard to close.

collisions.paper: He et al., 2025. AlphaDecay: Module-wise Weight Decay for Heavy-Tailed Balancing in LLMs. arXiv:2506.14562.
collisions.overlap: Both assign different weight decay strengths to different modules in LLMs. AlphaDecay uses spectral properties; this candidate uses alignment. Same application domain.

collisions.paper: Chen et al., 2025. Cautious Weight Decay. arXiv:2510.12402.
collisions.overlap: CWD already demonstrated on language model pre-training at billion scale with AdamW, Lion, and Muon. This candidate targets the same setting.

collisions.paper: Wang & Aitchison, 2024. How to set AdamW's weight decay as you scale model and dataset size. arXiv:2405.13698.
collisions.overlap: Studies optimal WD scaling for AdamW with transformers. Different focus but same application domain.

candidates.differentiation_notes: Competitive landscape is crowded. AlphaDecay already does module-wise adaptive WD for LLMs. CWD already handles alignment-based WD modulation for LLM pre-training. This candidate lacks convergence theory. Needs clear demonstration that per-layer alignment dynamics provide better signal than AlphaDecay's spectral analysis, and results at meaningful scale (>350M).

critical_citation_list: Sun et al. (CVPR 2025) — Foundation paper, alignment quantity delta_T
Chen et al. (2025) — Cautious Weight Decay, sign-based alignment, arXiv:2510.12402
Ghiasi et al. (NeurIPS 2023) — Adaptive WD by gradient/weight norm ratio
Xie et al. (NeurIPS 2023) — Scheduled Weight Decay by gradient norm
Nakamura & Hong (IEEE Access 2019) — AdaDecay, per-param adaptive WD
He et al. (2025) — AlphaDecay, module-wise WD for LLMs, arXiv:2506.14562
Jiang et al. (2025) — GALA, gradient alignment for LR adaptation, arXiv:2506.08419
Chou (2025) — WD proportional to gamma^2, arXiv:2512.08217
Hardt et al. (ICML 2016) — Uniform stability framework
Wan et al. (NeurIPS 2021) — Spherical Motion Dynamics
